frameworks.name: second-order image statistics
frameworks.one_line: low-level spectral/covariance regularities of natural images as scene-category code

frameworks.name: feedforward scene categorization
frameworks.one_line: rapid category readout from low-level features without segmentation

frameworks.name: top-down contextual gating of object recognition
frameworks.one_line: scene gist used as early context to guide object localization

core_question: Do category-correlated low-level image statistics support feedforward scene categorization that constrains subsequent object processing?
key_assumptions: image categories carry diagnostic second-order statistical regularities
scene-level information can be computed before grouping or segmentation
low-level features suffice to provide early contextual priors for objects
manipulations: image categories (scene types and object content)
presence vs absence of objects in scenes
key_findings: second-order statistics correlate with image categories, scene scale, and object content
scene categorization can be computed feedforward from low-level features
simple image statistics predict object presence/absence prior to exploration
low-level categorization benefits object localization and identification
interpretations: scene gist computed from image statistics provides early top-down/contextual signal in the visual hierarchy
categorization without segmentation is a viable computational route for guiding object recognition
